query: What detailed fees are associated with each decision point in the NYISO generation interconnection process based on the NYISO process manuals 23 and guide UG21?
final_answer:
I now have comprehensive information from both the NYISO Manual 23 (TEI Manual) and UG-21 to provide a detailed, authoritative answer. Let me compile this into a thorough response.

---

## NYISO Generation Interconnection Fees by Decision Point
### Based on Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion & Interconnection Manual) and UG-21 (Transmission Expansion & Interconnection User's Guide)

NYISO operates **two parallel interconnection frameworks** for generation projects: the **legacy Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** (still applicable to Class Year 2023 and prior projects under OATT Attachments S, X, and Z) and the **new Standard Interconnection Procedures / Cluster Study Process** (OATT Attachment HH, effective May 2024 per FERC Order 2023 compliance). Each has distinct fee structures at each decision point.

---

## PART 1 — LEGACY LARGE FACILITY INTERCONNECTION PROCEDURES (LFIP)
*(Manual 23, Section 3.3 / Figure 3; OATT Attachment X)*

### 🔵 Decision Point 1 — Interconnection Request Submission

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Application Fee** | **$10,000** | ❌ Non-refundable | Split 50/50 between NYISO and Connecting Transmission Owner (CTO) |
| **Initial Study Deposit (Feasibility)** | **$30,000** | ✅ Refundable (true-up to actuals) | Applied toward actual costs of the Optional Interconnection Feasibility Study (FES) |
| **Site Control Deposit** (if site control not yet demonstrated) | **+$10,000** | ✅ Refundable if site control demonstrated within cure period; otherwise non-refundable | An additional deposit in lieu of site control documentation |

> **Trigger:** Developer submits a completed Interconnection Request (Appendix 1 to LFIP) for each proposed site.

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 2 — Optional Interconnection Feasibility Study (FES / OFES)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **FES Study Costs (true-up)** | **Actual cost** | Partial refund/additional charge applies | $30,000 deposit applied against actual time-and-materials costs. Any excess is refunded; any shortfall is invoiced |

> **Purpose:** Preliminary assessment of interconnection impacts, violations, and estimated upgrade costs. Not mandatory but commonly pursued. NYISO targets completion within **45 business days**.

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 3 — System Reliability Impact Study (SRIS)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **SRIS Study Deposit** | **$30,000** | ✅ Refundable (true-up to actuals) | Separate deposit required for the SRIS; covers NYISO and CTO engineering hours |
| **Actual SRIS Study Costs** | **Billed at actuals** | Deposit is applied; over/under settled | Computed on time-and-materials basis; targets 120-day completion |

> **Decision by Developer:** After the SRIS, the developer decides whether to proceed toward Class Year. If the SRIS identifies needed upgrades, the developer must decide to accept ERIS (energy right) or CRIS (capacity right) path.

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 4 — Class Year Study Entry & Part 1

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Class Year Study Deposit (Part 1)** | **Set per Class Year notice** | ✅ Refundable (applied to study costs) | Covers NYISO and CTO costs for Part 1 reliability analysis of the full Class Year cohort |
| **Actual Class Year Part 1 Study Costs** | **Pro-rata actuals** | Applied against deposit | Allocated proportionally across all Class Year participants |

> **Purpose:** Part 1 identifies cost allocations for Connecting Transmission Owner Attachment Facilities (CTOAF) and System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs) needed for ERIS.

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 5 — Class Year Initial Decision Period (ERIS/SUF Acceptance)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Security for SUF/CTOAF Costs** | **100% of allocated upgrade costs** | Partially refundable as construction proceeds | Cash or letter of credit to secure developer's allocated share of System Upgrade Facilities |

> **Decision by Developer:** Within the **30-calendar-day Initial Decision Period**, developer issues either:
> - **Acceptance Notice** → posts security and proceeds to Class Year Part 2 / Deliverability Study
> - **Non-Acceptance Notice** → withdraws and forfeits deposits applied to study costs

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 6 — Class Year Part 2 / Deliverability Study (CRIS Decision)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Class Year Part 2 Deposit** | **Set per Class Year notice** | ✅ Refundable (applied to actuals) | Covers NYISO/CTO costs for the deliverability analysis identifying SDU needs |
| **Security for SDU Costs** | **100% of allocated System Deliverability Upgrade costs** | Partially refundable as construction proceeds | Required to obtain Capacity Resource Interconnection Service (CRIS) rights |

> **Typical SDU cost range per Class Year 2021:** $468–$2,557/kW of UCAP; Class Year 2023 identified $2.8B total in CTOAF + SUF costs ranging from $0–$581/kW (avg. $207/kW).

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 7 — Additional SDU Study (if applicable)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Additional SDU Study Deposit** | **Project-specific** | ✅ Applied to study actuals | Applies when Long Island or other constrained areas require a separate SDU study |

---

### 🔵 Decision Point 8 — Class Year Facilities Study

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Facilities Study Deposit** | **Actual costs basis** | ✅ Refundable (true-up) | Detailed engineering design study; costs borne by the developer |

---

### 🔵 Withdrawal Penalties (LFIP)

| Withdrawal Stage | Penalty |
|-----------------|---------|
| Before any study begins | Forfeit non-refundable application fee ($10,000); return unused deposits |
| After study commenced | Pay NYISO and CTO for all prudently incurred costs to date |
| After security posted for upgrades | Forfeit forfeited security portions per OATT; residual costs reconciled |

---

## PART 2 — NEW STANDARD INTERCONNECTION PROCEDURES / CLUSTER STUDY PROCESS
*(Manual 23, Section 3 [pending full revision]; UG-21 v2.0–v3.0; OATT Attachment HH, effective May 2024)*

### 🟢 Decision Point 1 — Application Window Submission

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Application Fee** | **$10,000** (Interconnection Request) or **$5,000** (CRIS-Only Request) | ❌ Non-refundable | Cash only; 75% to NYISO, 25% to CTO/ATOs |
| **Study Deposit** (based on project size): | | ✅ Applied against actual study costs | |
| — Facility < 80 MW | **$100,000** | ✅ | |
| — Facility 80–199 MW | **$150,000** | ✅ | |
| — Facility ≥ 200 MW | **$250,000** | ✅ | |
| — CRIS-Only projects | **$50,000** | ✅ | |
| **Site Control** | Full demonstration required | N/A | Technology-specific acreage requirements |

> **Trigger:** Developer submits during the **Application Window** (a limited open window, e.g., 75 business days for the Transition Cluster Study in Aug–Oct 2024). Full site control and technical data required upfront.

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 2 — Pre-Application (Optional, Rolling)

| Fee | Amount | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Pre-Application Request Fee** | **$5,000 per Point of Interconnection** | 25% to NYISO; 75% to Connecting TO/ATOs. Max 2 POIs per request. Optional heat-map information request |

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 3 — Customer Engagement Window / Physical Infeasibility Screen

| Fee | Amount | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Penalty-Free Withdrawal Window** | $0 | Developer may withdraw without penalty within **5 business days** following publication of the cluster project list if physical infeasibility is found |
| **Early Withdrawal Penalty** (after 5-day window) | **25% of Study Deposit** | Applies if developer withdraws before Phase 1 begins |

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 4 — Entry into Phase 1 (Reliability Study)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** | **$4,000/MW of nameplate capacity** | ✅ Applied toward upgrade costs at conclusion | Commercial readiness demonstration; posted prior to Phase 1 commencement |

> **Phase 1 scope:** Identifies CTOAFs, local SUFs, and Distribution Upgrades (analogous to Class Year Part 1). Targets completion within a defined milestone.

**Withdrawal Penalty during Phase 1:**
| Penalty Component | Amount |
|--|--|
| Study Deposit forfeited | **50% of Study Deposit** |
| RD1 forfeited | **10% of RD1** |

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 5 — Entry into Phase 2 (Deliverability Study)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)** | **Greater of: 20% of upgrade costs estimated in Phase 1, OR $4,000/MW** | ✅ Applied toward upgrade security | Commercial readiness to proceed to deliverability analysis (identifies SDUs) |

> **Phase 2 scope:** Determines SDU requirements and final cost allocations for CRIS. Analogous to Class Year Part 2.

**Withdrawal Penalty during Phase 2:**
| Penalty Component | Amount |
|--|--|
| Study Deposit forfeited | **100% of Study Deposit** |
| RD2 forfeited | **20% of RD2** |

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 6 — Post-Phase 2 / Final Acceptance (Security Posting)

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Security Deposit for Upgrade Costs** | **100% of estimated SUF + SDU costs** | Partial refund as construction progresses | Required at conclusion of Phase 2 to maintain CRIS and ERIS rights |

---

### 🟢 Decision Point 7 — Facility Modification Request (if applicable)

| Fee | Amount | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Modification Study Deposit** | **$10,000** | Required for any post-execution facility modification (except name changes, POI changes per Sec. 40.6.3.1, or COD extensions per Sec. 40.6.3.4) |

---

## PART 3 — TRANSMISSION INTERCONNECTION PROCEDURES (TIP)
*(Manual 23, Section 2.3 / Figure 1 — for transmission projects)*

| Study Phase | Deposit Amount |
|------------|---------------|
| **Application Fee** | **$10,000** non-refundable |
| **Optional Feasibility Study (OFES)** | **$60,000** deposit |
| **System Impact Study (SIS)** | **$120,000** deposit |
| **Facilities Study** | **$100,000** deposit |

> Transmission developers pay **actual study costs** under all phases; deposits are applied against invoiced actuals with true-up.

---

## Summary Comparison Table

| Decision Point | Legacy LFIP | New Cluster Study (Att. HH) |
|---|---|---|
| Application | $10,000 (non-refundable) | $10,000 / $5,000 CRIS-only (non-refundable) |
| Initial Study Deposit | $30,000 (feasibility) | $100K–$250K (by size) / $50K CRIS-only |
| Site Control Substitute Deposit | +$10,000 | Full site control required upfront |
| SRIS/Phase 1 Entry | $30,000 deposit | RD1 = $4,000/MW |
| Class Year / Phase 2 Entry | Project-specific | RD2 = max(20% Phase 1 costs, $4,000/MW) |
| SUF/SDU Security (Upgrade Costs) | 100% of allocated costs | 100% of estimated SUF + SDU costs |
| Withdrawal Penalty (early) | Forfeit applied study costs | 25–50% study deposit + 10–20% RD |
| Withdrawal Penalty (mid-study) | All prudently incurred costs | 100% study deposit + 20% RD2 |
| Facility Modification | N/A | $10,000 deposit |
| Pre-Application (optional) | N/A | $5,000/POI |

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Costs Have Escalated Significantly**: The new Cluster Study process requires **$100K–$250K study deposits** upfront versus $30K under the legacy LFIP — reflecting FERC Order 2023's emphasis on reducing speculative queue entries.
2. **Commercial Readiness Deposits Are New**: RD1 ($4,000/MW) and RD2 (≥20% of estimated upgrade costs) are brand-new financial commitment layers that did not exist in the legacy LFIP process.
3. **Withdrawal Penalties Are Now Explicit**: The Cluster Study introduces tiered forfeiture penalties (25–100% of study deposit + 10–20% of readiness deposits) at each decision point, designed to deter non-serious developers from clogging the queue.
4. **Legacy Projects Grandfathered**: Projects in Class Year 2023 and earlier continue under LFIP; the Cluster Study applies to new projects entering after the Transition Cluster Study opened (August 2024).
5. **Upgrade Cost Allocations Are Project-Specific**: The largest financial obligation — security for SUF/SDU upgrade costs — is entirely project-specific and can range from $0 to over $500/kW of nameplate capacity depending on the Class Year study results.

> ⚠️ **Disclaimer:** NYISO continues to revise Manual 23 to fully incorporate the Cluster Study requirements. For the most current fee schedules, developers should refer to the live versions at [nyiso.com/interconnections](https://www.nyiso.com/interconnections) and OATT Attachment HH directly.

## PART 2 — NEW STANDARD INTERCONNECTION PROCEDURES / CLUSTER STUDY PROCESS

### 🟢 Decision Point 1 — Application Window Submission

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------------|-------|
| **Application Fee** | **$10,000** (Interconnection Request) or **$5,000** (CRIS-Only Request) | ❌ Non-refundable | Cash only; 75% to NYISO, 25% to CTO/ATOs |
| **Study Deposit** (based on project size): | | ✅ Applied against actual study costs | |

| Penalty Component | Amount |

| RD1 forfeited | **10% of RD1** |

| Fee | Amount | Refundable? | Notes |

| Decision Point | Legacy LFIP | New Cluster Study (Att. HH) |
